_note: R6 is a structural/symbolic result: Δt_obs/Δt_emit = (1+z). Most checks are symbolic. Numerical inputs below are used for (a) DES 2024 comparison and (b) the v_emit/v_obs high-precision check that exercises the speed-of-causality principle.

c.notes: Photons travel at v < c strictly. Scripts must NOT set v_photon = c.

eV_to_J.notes: Used to convert m_gamma between kg and eV/c^2.

m_gamma_kg.source: GCM estimate (L1 claim). Used to compute v = c·sqrt(1 - (m c^2/E)^2).

des_2024_alpha_central.source: Dark Energy Survey 2024, arXiv:2406.05050 — SNe Ia light-curve time dilation scaling (1+z)^α.

r6_alpha.notes: Exact consequence of L3 + redshift definition + information propagation at v.

z_examples.source: Illustrative redshift values for limit-case checks.
z_examples.notes: z=0 (no dilation), z=0.1 (small), z=1 (SNe Ia scale), z=10/100 (high-z limit).

E_visible_photon_eV.source: Reference energy for a visible-light photon.
E_visible_photon_eV.notes: Used in the v_emit/v_obs ratio calculation at 60-digit mpmath precision.

hypothetical_future_alpha_central.notes: Used in falsification-gate check to estimate when R6 would be excluded at >3σ.

hypothetical_future_alpha_sigma.source: Hypothetical LSST-precision σ (~2.5x tighter than DES 2024).
hypothetical_future_alpha_sigma.notes: With this σ and α=1.010, R6's α=1.000 would be excluded at 5σ.
